frameworks.name: Exit Trigger Framework
frameworks.description: Exits made under duress produce poor outcomes. Pre-defined exit triggers — set when thinking is clear — prevent both premature exit (noise) and delayed exit (denial). The trigger is decided before it is needed.
steps: Define exit triggers for each major commitment: what conditions would definitively indicate it is time to leave?
Separate lagging indicators (consequences already here) from leading indicators (early signals before full consequences arrive)
Set both floor triggers (conditions bad enough that exit is mandatory) and ceiling triggers (goals achieved, time to move to next level)
Write the triggers down: unwritten triggers are vulnerable to rationalization under pressure
Review triggers periodically: conditions and goals change; triggers should update accordingly
Define the exit process: not just when to exit, but how — sequence of steps to transition out cleanly
antipatterns: No pre-defined triggers — forces real-time decision-making under emotional and financial pressure
Only floor triggers, no ceiling triggers — misses the signal that it is time to harvest and move on
Triggers set too high — by the time they are met, options for clean exit are exhausted

frameworks.name: Clean Exit Architecture
frameworks.description: The quality of an exit determines the quality of what comes next. Clean exits preserve relationships, reputation, and optionality. Burned exits close options that are not yet visible.
steps: Principle: exit as if you will interact with these people again — you almost certainly will
Transition timeline: give appropriate notice, document what you know, train successors where possible
Relationship preservation: identify the 3-5 relationships from this context worth maintaining actively — exit the role, not the relationships
Reputation audit: what will be said about how you left? Design the exit to produce the reputation you want.
Financial close: ensure all financial obligations are resolved before exit — incomplete financial exits create ongoing entanglement
Narrative design: define your own story about why you left before others define it for you
antipatterns: Burning the exit — closing options that are not yet visible in frustration with current conditions
No relationship preservation plan — exiting the context and all relationships simultaneously
No narrative design — allowing others to define the exit story

frameworks.name: Next Chapter Architecture
frameworks.description: Exits without destinations produce drift. The transition between what you are leaving and what you are building is where identity and momentum are most vulnerable. Pre-built next chapter prevents the gap.
steps: Do not exit into a vacuum: before leaving, have minimum viable clarity on the next direction
Identify the overlap period: what is the minimum viable next chapter you can build while still in the current chapter?
Financial bridge: calculate runway — how long can you operate after exit before the next chapter must generate income?
Identity bridge: your identity is not your current role — define who you are outside the role before you leave it
Quick wins in new chapter: what can you accomplish in the first 30 days after exit to build momentum and validate direction?
Re-entry option: if exit proves premature, what is the re-entry path? Knowing this reduces the psychological cost of exiting
antipatterns: Exiting before minimum viable next chapter is defined — maximizes drift risk
No financial bridge calculation — forces premature monetization of next chapter before it is ready
Identity attached entirely to current role — exit produces identity crisis, not just role change

prompts.name: Exit Trigger Design
prompts.template: Design exit triggers for: [commitment — job, client, partnership, business, relationship type]. Current conditions: [describe]. Goals I came here to achieve: [describe]. Give me floor triggers (conditions bad enough to mandate exit), ceiling triggers (goals achieved, time to move on), and leading indicators for each.

prompts.name: Clean Exit Plan
prompts.template: Design a clean exit plan from: [context]. Timeline I am considering: [describe]. Key relationships to preserve: [list]. Financial obligations to close: [describe]. Reputation I want to leave: [describe]. Give me the exit sequence, relationship preservation plan, and narrative design.

prompts.name: Next Chapter Architecture
prompts.template: Build my next chapter architecture. What I am exiting: [describe]. What I am moving toward (even if unclear): [describe]. Financial runway after exit: [months]. Identity outside current role: [describe or help me define]. Design my overlap period, financial bridge, and 30-day momentum plan.

examples.title: Executive Exiting Corporate Role for Entrepreneurship
examples.context: VP at a corporation, 8 years tenure, planning to launch independent consulting practice
examples.exit_triggers_set: Floor: Role scope reduced OR new leadership misaligned with values. Ceiling: 18-month consulting pipeline established AND $200K ARR validated.
examples.clean_exit_design: 6-month notice period. Documented all institutional knowledge. Maintained relationships with 12 key former colleagues. Exited with full support of leadership.
examples.next_chapter: Built consulting client pipeline during final 6 months at company. Had 3 signed clients on day 1 of independence. Financial bridge: 18 months runway.
examples.outcome: Year 1 consulting revenue: $380K. Zero identity crisis — had defined independent identity 2 years before exit.

vocabulary.definition: A pre-defined condition that indicates it is time to leave a commitment. Set when thinking is clear to prevent rationalization under pressure.
A minimum threshold — conditions bad enough to mandate exit regardless of sunk cost or fear.
A maximum threshold — goals achieved or potential realized, indicating it is time to harvest gains and move to the next level.
A transition designed to preserve relationships, reputation, and optionality. Contrasted with burned exit, which closes future options.
The capital reserve covering expenses between exiting one income source and establishing the next. The runway that enables unhurried transition.
The phase when the next chapter is being built while still in the current one. Minimizes the gap and allows validation before full commitment.
Deliberately crafting the story of why you left before others define it. Reputation is built in exits, not just entrances.

sources.title: Transitions
sources.relevance: Transition architecture, endings before beginnings, identity through change

sources.title: Linchpin
sources.relevance: Identity outside role, indispensability as portable asset

sources.title: The Dip
sources.relevance: Strategic quitting vs. failure quitting, exit timing

v78_consciousnesses: Ancient One v3.0 (temporal evaluation, 10-year horizon post-exit)
Lucifer v3.0 (fear-based delay of necessary exit)
Meta-2.6 Decision Architecture (exit as Type 1 decision requiring full analysis)
master_laws: Law 30 (Exit — always have a way out)
Law 3 (Agency — exit capability is structural agency)
Law 9 (Time — delayed necessary exits are expensive)
Law 25 (Negotiation — willingness to exit is negotiating power)
sovereign_needs_served: Agency (primary — exit capability is the ultimate expression of agency)
Security (clean exits preserve financial and reputational security)
Growth (timely exits free capital and attention for compounding opportunities)

4_gate_validation.high_leverage: PASS — exit capability is a force multiplier on all other negotiations and commitments
